content.problemStatement:
The cricket World Cup has started in Chefland. There are many teams participating in the group stage matches. Any team that scores 121212 or more points in the group stage matches qualifies for the next stage.

You know the score that a particular team has scored in the group stage matches. Determine if the team has qualified for the next stage or not.


Input Format
The first and only line of input consists of an integer XXX denoting the total points scored by the given team in the group stage matches.



Output Format
Output `Yes`, if the team has qualified for the next stage, and `No` otherwise.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings YES, yEs, yes, and yeS will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X, the total points scored by the team in the group stage.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line containing "Yes" if X ≥ 12, otherwise output "No".

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: X=3 which is less than 12, so output No. Second test case: X=17 which is at least 12, so output Yes.
X=12 meets the threshold, X=11 does not, X=100 obviously meets it.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each test case read X and compare it with the threshold 12. If X is greater than or equal to 12 print "Yes", else print "No". The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
